output:
## Solution

**Yes.** Observing uvzi makes it more likely that zuph occurred.

**Given:**
- P(zuph) = 0.77
- P(¬zuph ∧ uvzi) = 0.11
- P(zuph ∧ uvzi) = 0.46

**Step 1 — Total probability of uvzi:**

P(uvzi) = P(zuph ∧ uvzi) + P(¬zuph ∧ uvzi) = 0.46 + 0.11 = **0.57**

**Step 2 — Posterior via Bayes:**

P(zuph | uvzi) = P(zuph ∧ uvzi) / P(uvzi) = 0.46 / 0.57 ≈ **0.8070**

P(¬zuph | uvzi) = 0.11 / 0.57 ≈ **0.1930**

**Step 3 — Compare posterior to prior:**

| | Probability |
|---|---|
| Prior P(zuph) | 0.77 |
| Posterior P(zuph \| uvzi) | 0.8070 |
| Shift | +0.037 |

Observing uvzi raises the probability of zuph from 77% to ~80.7%. The answer is **yes** — if uvzi occurs, zuph is more likely to have occurred (both in absolute terms at ~81%, and relative to the prior of 77%).
